reviews.text:
TOTAL SCAMMERS!  On 16 February, I booked an appointment with dermatologist Dr. Geoffrey Brent for Friday the 20th. However, immediately after completing the booking, I received a phone call from the clinic informing me that the doctor was not available on the 20th and that the earliest available appointment was actually on the 23rd.
It appears that the doctor’s schedule was not up to date in the clinic’s booking system. When I made the reservation, several time slots were shown as available for the 20th. However, during the phone call, I was informed that there was no availability at all on that day. As a result, the clinic cancelled my appointment. And shortly after the call , I received an email confirming the cancellation. So basically ,booking appointment, receiving call from the clinic to cancel, then receiving the cancellation email ; they all happened in less than an hour.
The following day, I noticed that I had nevertheless been charged for the cancelled appointment. Since then, I have been trying to get a refund, but for some reason the clinic’s accounts/credit department has not processed it.
They are withholding people’s money without any valid reason!!! It’s been a week I am still trying to get my £275!!!
AVOID FROM THIS CLINIC! I will be warning everyone to avoid this clinic at all costs!
If my refund is not processed within 48 hours (the legal deadline), I will begin formal action to recover my money. As a first step, I will file an official complaint through ADR. I will also report the matter to Trading Standards. If necessary, I am also fully prepared to pursue the claim through the SCC as well.

I had a very disappointing experience with LIPS Healthcare due to poor communication and lack of clarity throughout the process.

When I first called, I was told I could receive the service and have a doctor consultation afterwards without needing to book an appointment. To be safe, I called again on the day to double check, but the staff sounded unsure and quickly booked me an appointment instead, which already caused confusion.

When I arrived at the clinic, there were very few instructions and no clear explanation of what to expect. I felt confused and anxious the entire time. I was then informed that there was no doctor available on site, which completely contradicted what I had been told previously.

Although I tried to remain calm and trust their professionalism, the information provided was unclear and inconsistent. When I later emailed to ask for clarification, I was told that this explanation was not included and that I would need to pay for an additional service just to have my results explained.

Overall, the experience felt unprofessional, poorly managed, and extremely stressful. I would not recommend this clinic to anyone who values clear communication and reliable healthcare guidance.

Avoid. Misleading booking and poor patient care.

I booked this appointment solely to have a small bump removed from my eyelid. I was clearly told over the phone that this would be done on the day, and I reconfirmed it on the call before attending.

When I arrived, I received nothing more than a five-minute eye test, completely irrelevant to why I was there, and was sent away without treatment.

When I questioned this, the Dr Maan Kasmiya simply said no and ended the appointment.

I was then invoiced £165 for an appointment that provided no care, no solution, and no value.

Despite sending emails explaining I had been misled at the point of booking, I received no response whatsoever, only repeated payment chasers until I was effectively bullied into paying.

This was a complete waste of time and money, and the lack of communication afterward was unacceptable.

Extremely disappointing experience.

I booked a blood test for my mother a month ago and pre-paid a £147. I then cancelled the test due to some personal reasons. I was told there was a cancellation fees of £30, but the remaining amount of £117 would be refunded. It's been a month and have not refunded me anything yet. They even took my card details two weeks ago and sent me a slip confirming that the refund was processed. I even called my bank but they said nothing came through. It does not take more than 2 weeks for refunds to get processed in the UK. They are a bunch of unprofessional scammers. I am still fighting for my refund. Please be careful. Do not pay anything beforehand.

Recently I had a negative experience with neurologist Dr. Gratwicke. I was prescribed an expensive (£600), strong migraine medication despite having long-standing neurological hypersensitivity (my nervous system can’t tolerate even caffeine contents of a single cup of tea) and symptoms that did not clearly fit a migraine pattern. I specifically asked about the certainty of the diagnosis and was assured it was correct.

After taking the medication, I experienced severe adverse effects — chest heaviness, breathing difficulties, throat constriction, and acute neurological tension — requiring an A&E visit and leaving me unable to work for a week, with lingering effects for weeks afterward.

It then took 25 days to receive a response from the consultant. The reply stated that my symptoms were “quite unusual” and “might not be related,” even though shortness of breath is listed as a known side effect of the medication occurring in 1 in 100 people that requires immediate medical attention.

My concerns relate to clinical judgment, a mismatch between my symptoms and the diagnosis, slow communication, and a lack of recognition of the seriousness of what happened.

Clinic never responded to my subsequent customer experience email.

Update after customer complaints response: they looked into it, found no breach of prescribing policy and never responded to my email asking what steps they'll take to implement changes that would: 1) improve communication around diagnostic uncertainty, 2) ensure patients with known hypersensitivity have risks discussed in a transparent, thorough, and reassuring manner and 3) ensure timely follow-ups when patients report significant side effects.

Booked GP visit online and paid for it online. Recieved a call later to say the time is not available, need to be rescheduled. Received the call again later to be told the slot was already gone need to be rescheduled again. Another call later asking to make a payment via phone, my baby was loudly crying and I said I can’t do it and will do at the clinic, the PA was still pushing me to pay via email. As I booked few days ago I didn’t remember I already made the payment online at the time of booking. They took payment at reception before the appointment. Two days after the visit still haven’t been sent the invoice, referrals discussed with GP or the refund. Very pushy to get the payment, very slow and chaotic with the actual service . Disappointing.

Unfortunately I have been let down by the consultants at this clinic.  One through no fault of his own and the other through administrative incompetence.  I need a PRP injection in my right arm and unfortunately the first consultant I saw is currently signed off sick and unable to perform the injection.  I discovered this a month after making the appointment. I was recommended a different consultant whom I arranged an appointment with for today, 3 weeks after first contact.  I have travelled to London specifically for this appointment only to be informed this morning that the alternative  consultant DOESN'T DO PRP INJECTIONS!!!  Why did these two consultants not confer and establish this fact three weeks ago? My time and discomfort is clearly of no interest to them whatsoever.

DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE!

It's truly the worst I've ever used including the NHS, despite it being private.
Having been told by the consultant that I was going to be put in a knee brace ASAP (it will take a week, two max, they said). I then have to chase multiple times, emailing and calling them, and 2 weeks later they say: we have no information about a knee brace being ordered, we'll give you a call by the end of this week to clarify what's going on.

So that's 3 weeks just to receive a phone call! I've had a torn PCL which has meant I cannot go into work. I was told it needed to be put in a brace as soon as possible. I tell them this and they say, 'we'll give you a call by the end of this week'. It's Tuesday today... It's outrageous.

I can't believe how poorly this has been handled and the lack of urgency from their side (which could have massive implications on people's long term health) is staggering.

Stay away and find a clinic that takes your health seriously.

Arrogant, unprofessional, and utterly careless toward patients — and the list goes on.

I visited this clinic less than two months ago with a minor back injury and nearly ended up being pushed toward unnecessary surgery.

A word of advice to the leadership team: instead of spending on PAs and superficial frills, consider hiring competent staff who can actually diagnose and support patients properly.

As a local resident, I was genuinely excited to have a clinic so close by, but the experience turned out to be profoundly disappointing. I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone.

THE WORST NEUROSURGEON AND PHYSIOTHERAPIST — yes, every letter deservedly in capitals.

This community deserves far better care and far more accountability than what this clinic currently offers.

I came to buy medicine at this pharmacy when my GP told me it was the same price to buy it as it was prescribed. This pharmacy was selling the medicine at over £30 - when I questioned this, they informed me it was this price everywhere and I wasn't going to get a better deal. I trusted them and bought it. I later discovered that at EVERY pharmacy in London it is between £11-14.
Furious at this I left a two star review, rating the pharmacy as overpriced.
The manager of this pharmacy searched my name from the review and proceeded TO COME TO MY WORK PLACE to tell me to take it down. This was deeply uncomfortable and unprofessional - and caused friction with my management. They pressured me into taking down the post and I was left feeling embarrassed and harassed.
I ended up reporting the pharmacy to the board of Pharmacies in the UK. They informed me the behaviour was unacceptable but there was nothing they could do.
The whole experience was shocking.
I'm only putting my review back up because I have moved jobs to one no longer within walking distance. Do not go here.
They only dispense private prescriptions which they did not state on the phone when I called. Wasted journey.
Would avoid- the way they followed up to receive payment was awful. Wouldnt respond, payment links that didnt work and threatened debt collection.

Would give 0 stars if I could. Receptionists very rude. Made me wait so long for the first appointment they couldn’t finish it so made to come back for another. Then tried to make me pay a scam charge for something they had never mentioned. …

I visited the LIPS store inside Battersea Power Station (the pharmacy/clinic space on the upper floor) and I am genuinely appalled by how they treat customers. It was very unprofessional and rude and because of that I didn’t bother wasting …
They have low range of product, almost everything I wanted is missing, do not recommend

In September, I had an appointment with Dr Lau about a cut finger. Looking back, I should have gone to a regular A&E but I was new to Battersea and didn’t know where else to go. While the customer service was nice, it was clear that this clinic is very money-focused and didn’t pay attention to treating my injury properly. They charged me around £300 for the treatment and antibiotics, which I thought was excessive. In the end, I had to go to my home country to get a proper treatment (for free!), because the one that was given to me at LIPS was very bad and could have resulted in me having lasting consequences.

I’d like to first of all say that my review does not reflect the space itself which is very clean or the people who were mostly respectful and nice BUT I have to stand up for myself as a paying individual against misinformation and misleading people when it comes to health costs. I went to LIPS for an ENT appointment. At the time of the appointment, Dr. Wong offered to perform a small procedure (I believe it was a nasal endoscopy but I’m not sure) which took about 5-10 mins. When I inquired as to the cost (as one does because private medical is very costly), he said somewhere in the £150-200 range and I said OK. At the end of the appointment, we settled the price at £495 at the Lips front desk, the initial consultation fee along with the cost of the additional procedure and I paid this the day of the appointment in July 2025 which I confirmed with the Lips team. Feb 2026 (7 MONTHS LATER) I am receiving notice of another £510 facility fee. I expressed my confusion to both Lips and Dr. Wongs team as this was never disclosed to me as an additional cost at the time of the appointment and I am only now alerted to this cost in writing 7 months after my appointment. Had I been notified of a facility fee in addition to the procedure cost I already paid of £495, I would have never agreed to the procedure or even the appointment. I feel very mislead and upset with the whole situation and will say not be returning to LIPS again as I feel they were not cost transparent at all which is so necessary when so many medical facilities can be so predatory towards scared individuals seeking help and assurance.

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. At LIPS Healthcare, we take all concerns seriously and are keen to understand what went wrong. Please reach out to us at +442071646114 or via email at complaints@lips.org.uk so we can address your concerns directly and work towards a resolution.

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. At LIPS Healthcare, we take all concerns seriously and are keen to understand what went wrong. Please reach out to us at … More

BUPA Clients Beware – Ask for Costs in Advance and Check Your Emails for debt agency threats!

Dr Emma was lovely and the facilities are modern and fresh, but sadly my experience with the administration and billing process was disappointing.

I was told my BUPA policy would cover both the consultation and cryotherapy treatment, yet I was later charged an additional £103 without this being clearly explained beforehand.

To make matters worse, I later discovered an email in my spam folder threatening debt collection action over an alleged outstanding balance of just £43.60. Given that the clinic had my home address, a formal letter would have been better.

A friend of mine also had a similar experience, believing treatment would be covered by BUPA only to later discover additional costs after providing payment details.

Overall, the impression was very much “take payment first, explain later,” which left me feeling uncomfortable and unable to trust the billing process.

Actual in-person treatment and service are good and very personable, but dealing with the admin branch of this company is a nightmare. Was told that my appointments would be fully covered under insurance (AXA), only to bill me multiple times in the following months with zero clarity or explanation on what the payments are for. Can't even pay online on their portal, which has tech issues and keeps glitching. Unhappy.
Great clinic and very competent doctors, however waiting times are 30-90 mins, which is absolutely unacceptable given it’s a private practice.
Vibe above and beyond!
